title: Pre Scrub
problem: Ambulance claim denials are not random. They cluster on a small set of failure modes that are deterministic given the document: missing origin/destination modifiers (RH/SH/QL...), medical necessity narrative that doesn't justify the HCPCS A code chosen (A0427 ALS Emergency vs A0429 BLS Emergency), missing PCS (Physician Certification Statement) for non emergent transports, or a patient signature gap on the ePCR.
direction: An ambulance billing pre submission scrubber: predicts the denial reason from the closed ePCR + dispatch trace, points the medic at the missing field, and proves it would have cleared payer adjudication.
why: NEMSIS as the substrate is the non obvious technical bet. Every shop currently building "EMS billing AI" wires per vendor connectors. NEMSIS makes the integration set N=1.

archetypes.name: ambulance evidence replay
archetypes.trigger: ambulance signal changes while claim context is stale
archetypes.expected: block release until cited evidence is regenerated

archetypes.name: claim boundary probe
archetypes.trigger: claim handoff crosses a policy or trust boundary
archetypes.expected: route to reviewer with evidence packet

archetypes.name: denials regression harness
archetypes.trigger: denials behavior regresses against the last accepted fixture
archetypes.expected: open a regression issue with trace and benchmark delta

archetypes.name: random operator packet
archetypes.trigger: random output needs a human-readable audit packet
archetypes.expected: accept only if decision claims cite fixture evidence
